mapping.a_element: multi-atom cold ensemble used as the storage medium for light
mapping.b_element: cold-atom cloud used as the gain-plus-scattering medium for light

mapping.a_element: atom-light interaction that maps optical spatial modes onto collective atomic coherences
mapping.b_element: atom-light interaction (atomic polarizability of a pumped two-level atom) that provides gain and scattering

brief_justification: The only genuine overlap is the physical substrate (light interacting with a cold multi-atom ensemble); the transferable machinery differs. Paper A is a coherent, linear quantum-state storage/retrieval protocol maximizing multimode capacity with no threshold, whereas Paper B is a lasing-threshold/critical-phenomenon governed by a single critical parameter (on-resonance optical thickness b0) balancing gain against multiple-scattering losses, so no role-for-role mapping of the driving mechanism holds.
